---
title: "aisdk"
subtitle: "A production-grade AI toolkit for R"
toc: false
---

**aisdk** is a unified, layered AI toolkit for R. The self-contained core
package exposes a single `provider:model` interface over many AI providers,
plus agent / tool / skill base classes and robust request machinery. A family
of companion packages extends that core with extra providers, multi-agent
orchestration, the Model Context Protocol, a skill ecosystem, messaging
channels, charting/reporting, a Shiny web UI, an interactive terminal
console, and local model inference — each opt-in, so the core stays lean.

```r
# Install the family from GitHub (recommended while the companion packages
# make their way onto CRAN). install.packages("pak") first if you need it.
pak::pak("YuLab-SMU/aisdk")   # core only; see "Get started" for the full family

library(aisdk)
chat <- create_chat_session("openai:gpt-4o")
generate_text(chat, "Summarise the tidyverse in one sentence.")
```

See [**Get started**](getting-started.qmd) for a one-step install of the whole
family.

## The family

### Core

| Package | What it does |
|---|---|
| [**aisdk**](packages/aisdk.qmd) | Self-contained core: unified `provider:model` interface, agent/tool/skill base classes, request/retry machinery, and the shared model-setup and session-persistence layer used by every front end. |

### Providers & local inference

| Package | What it does |
|---|---|
| [**aisdk.providers**](packages/aisdk.providers.qmd) | Long-tail OpenAI-/Anthropic-compatible provider adapters: DeepSeek, Moonshot/Kimi, Stepfun, Volcengine, AiHubMix, xAI, OpenRouter, Bailian, NVIDIA. |
| [**aisdk.slm**](packages/aisdk.slm.qmd) | Local small language model inference: quantized GGUF and ONNX/torch models on local hardware, with model discovery and downloading. |

### Agents & orchestration

| Package | What it does |
|---|---|
| [**aisdk.orchestration**](packages/aisdk.orchestration.qmd) | Multi-agent layer: Flow (delegation tracing, guardrails), AgentTeam (Manager–Worker), and the Mission system (planning, DAG scheduling, retries, checkpointing). |

### Tools & ecosystem

| Package | What it does |
|---|---|
| [**aisdk.mcp**](packages/aisdk.mcp.qmd) | Model Context Protocol client and server: use tools from stdio/SSE MCP servers, and expose R functions as MCP tools. |
| [**aisdk.skills**](packages/aisdk.skills.qmd) | Skill authoring, a global skill store, skill-forge tools, and a bundled pack of ready-to-use skills. |

### Integrations & I/O

| Package | What it does |
|---|---|
| [**aisdk.channels**](packages/aisdk.channels.qmd) | Messaging-channel runtime and adapters (e.g. Feishu/Lark): webhooks, event processing, per-conversation sessions, document ingestion. |
| [**aisdk.datatools**](packages/aisdk.datatools.qmd) | Heavy-dependency charting and reporting: a ggplot2 chart schema/renderer and a knitr-based AI reporting engine. |
| [**aisdk.shiny**](packages/aisdk.shiny.qmd) | Shiny web chat interface and model-configuration UI for interactive, streaming conversations. |
| [**aisdk.console**](packages/aisdk.console.qmd) | Interactive terminal chat console: the `console_chat()` REPL with slash commands, inspector overlay, session branching, and the built-in terminal agent. |

## Design

The core (`aisdk`) is deliberately self-contained and the only package that
needs to be on CRAN for the basics to work. Everything else **registers
itself with the core on load** — providers into the provider registry, skills
into the skill registry, the ggplot coercion handler into the serializer — so
heavy or optional dependencies never reach the core unless you actually install
the companion. See [Get started](getting-started.qmd) for the typical setup.
